purpose: Argyle Trainer Dashboard is a web application designed for faculty members to manage and track their training activities and student progress.

primary_workflows.name: Faculty Login
primary_workflows.description: Faculty members log in to access their personalized dashboard and training data.

primary_workflows.name: View Dashboard Overview
primary_workflows.description: After login, users see a summary of key training metrics, recent activities, and student performance.

primary_workflows.name: Track Student Progress
primary_workflows.description: Faculty can view individual student progress, including completed modules, scores, and engagement levels.

primary_workflows.name: Manage Training Sessions
primary_workflows.description: Create, edit, and schedule training sessions for students, including setting deadlines and materials.

primary_workflows.name: Generate Reports
primary_workflows.description: Generate and export reports on training outcomes, student performance, and faculty activity.

dashboard_gaps: No visible quick-access to student alerts or at-risk indicators
Missing summary of upcoming deadlines or overdue tasks
navigation_issues: No navigation observed beyond login and home page; likely missing breadcrumbs or sidebar menu

user_types_observed.evidence: The product name 'Argyle Trainer Dashboard' and the login page suggest it is intended for trainers or faculty who manage training.
user_types_observed.primary_goal: Monitor and improve student training outcomes through data-driven insights and session management.

user_types_observed.evidence: The presence of reporting features and overall dashboard structure implies administrative oversight.
user_types_observed.primary_goal: Oversee faculty performance and institutional training metrics.

quality_concerns.concern: No crawl data available for pages beyond login and home; likely missing content or navigation structure.
No interaction data captured; may indicate poor event tracking or incomplete page rendering.
Only two endpoints returned 200; no indication of performance issues but limited data.
